news.summary: US stock futures are trading with mixed sentiment this morning. Flowers Foods, Inc. (NYSE: FLO) is anticipated to announce quarterly earnings of 38 cents per share on revenue totaling $1.60 billion before the opening bell. In after-hours trading, Flowers Foods shares saw a 0.1% drop, resting at $17.05. On the other hand, Applied Materials Inc (NASDAQ: AMAT) posted second-quarter earnings that surpassed expectations, although sales fell short of estimates. Applied Materials forecasts third-quarter revenue to be around $7.2 billion, with a possible variance of $500 million. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Stocks in focus for Friday: Flowers Foods (NYSE: FLO) expected to report earnings of 38 cents/share on $1.60B revenue. Applied Materials Inc (NASDAQ: AMAT) reported Q2 earnings, expecting Q3 revenue of $7.2B.

news.summary: The CNN Money Fear and Greed index reflected a decrease in overall market sentiment, despite remaining in the "Greed" zone. On Thursday, U.S. stocks had a mixed settlement, with the S&P 500 marking gains for the fourth consecutive session. Throughout the week, the S&P 500 saw an increase of approximately 4.5%, while the Dow rose by about 2.6%. Over the same period, the Nasdaq surged by roughly 6.6%. Major tech companies experienced significant gains, with Nvidia and Tesla each adding around 15%. Amazon's shares climbed by about 6%, while Alphabet saw a 7% increase week-to-date. Walmart Inc. reported better-than-expected earnings for Q1 on Thursday. For further details, refer to the article available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: S&P 500 rises for fourth session with tech stocks boost. Fear index remains in 'Greed' zone. S&P gains 4.5%, Nasdaq 6.6%. Notable gains of Nvidia (NVDA) and Tesla (TSLA) at 15%, Amazon (AMZN) at 6%, and more. Walmart (WMT) reports strong earnings.

news.summary: In Singapore, Hafnia Limited ("Hafnia" or "Company"), with OSE ticker code "HAFNI" and NYSE ticker code “HAFN”, disclosed financial data for Q1 2025 on 15 May 2025. An irregularity was identified in the Q1 2025 Earnings Report on Page 6, involving the misrepresentation of broker valuations for the fleet. The report erroneously included 100% value of the joint venture vessels instead of the accurate 50%. This discrepancy has prompted a reevaluation of the financial information shared.
news.headline: Hafnia Limited corrects fleet value presentation in Q1 2025 report after including 100% instead of stated 50% joint venture vessels.

United Solar, a top supplier of premium polysilicon solutions, is looking forward to this strategic partnership to enhance their presence in the renewable energy sector.
news.headline: United Solar Polysilicon partners with OQ Alternative Energy to develop a 700MW photovoltaic power station in Oman.

news.summary: The article outlines a bullish prediction for Bitcoin's price, suggesting it could reach $200,000 or higher this year, with potential to hit nearly $1 million by the end of the decade. The analysis is based on the interplay between Bitcoin and gold prices, with Bitcoin expected to follow gold to new all-time highs. The "power curve" model is used to measure Bitcoin's value in gold ounces, avoiding the inflationary nature of the US dollar. Additionally, if Bitcoin were to capture 50% of gold's market cap with gold hitting $5,000/oz by 2030, Bitcoin could potentially reach $924,000. The analysis also discusses Bitcoin's four-year price cycle and its potential for hitting $250,000 or more in 2025. The article emphasizes that all investment decisions involve risk, and readers should conduct their own research before acting.
news.headline: Bitcoin may hit $220K in 2025, with potential to reach $1 million by the decade's end, backed by gold-based forecast.

news.summary: CAVA Group Inc., a Mediterranean fast-casual chain, aims to have 1,000 restaurants by 2032, as announced during their first-quarter 2025 earnings call. The CEO highlighted the brand's success and emphasized their strategic vision despite economic uncertainties. In the first quarter, the company saw a 10.8% growth in same-restaurant sales, with a 7.5% increase in traffic. They opened 15 new restaurants, totaling 382. CEO Schulman reassured investors about potential supply chain impacts from Trump's tariffs, stating their exposure is limited due to domestically sourced ingredients. Full story on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: CAVA aims to have 1,000 restaurants by 2032 with 10.8% sales growth and 382 restaurants opened. CEO mentions limited tariff exposure.

news.summary: The global circulating oil system additives market is expected to grow significantly, jumping from around USD 2.8 billion in 2023 to approximately USD 5.1 billion by 2034, with a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 4.2%. The rise is attributed to increased demand for advanced lubrication solutions in sectors like automotive, industrial machinery, marine, and energy. Factors fueling this growth include technological advancements, stringent environmental regulations, and a shift towards sustainable practices. Market drivers include industrial growth, machinery advancements, evolution of the automotive industry towards electric/hybrid vehicles, and demands in the marine and energy sectors. However, fluctuations in raw material prices, environmental regulations, and technological complexities pose restraints. Various types of additives such as detergents, dispersants, antioxidants, and rust inhibitors are crucial for maintenance and protection purposes. Automotive applications dominate the market, followed by industrial, marine, and energy segments, each with specific requirements for additives to meet operational needs and regulations.
news.headline: Global circulating oil system additives market projected to grow from USD 2.8 billion in 2023 to around USD 5.1 billion by 2034, with a CAGR of 4.2%. Key drivers include demand from automotive, industrial machinery, marine, and energy sectors, driven by technological advancements and sustainable practices. Market restraints include raw material price volatility, environmental regulations, and technological complexity. Product types include detergents, dispersants, antioxidants, and rust inhibitors. Application segments include automotive, industrial, marine, and energy sectors.

news.summary: The global pipe belt conveyor market is expected to grow significantly, increasing from around USD 1.2 billion in 2024 to approximately USD 1.8 billion by 2030, with a CAGR of 6.0%. This growth is fueled by rising demand in mining, construction, and agriculture sectors, as well as advancements in automation technologies. Key drivers include efficient material handling solutions sought in mining and construction industries, automation integration to enhance efficiency and safety, and the agricultural industry's need for reliable transportation systems. However, market growth may be impeded by the high initial investment required for installation and maintenance, technical complexities in design and operation requiring skilled personnel, and stringent environmental regulations affecting material and technology choices. The market offers both portable and fixed pipe belt conveyors catering to different needs, and is segmented into applications such as mining, transportation, construction, and agriculture. The full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Global pipe belt conveyor market projected to grow from USD 1.2B in 2024 to USD 1.8B by 2030, with a CAGR of 6.0%. Key drivers include demand in mining, construction, and agriculture sectors, as well as automation advancements. Major restraints are high initial investment and technical complexities. Portable and fixed conveyors are key product types. Mining, transportation, construction, and agriculture are main application segments.

news.summary: The global digital photosensitive material market is set to increase from USD 6.2 billion in 2024 to approximately USD 10.4 billion by 2034, demonstrating a 5.4% CAGR. Factors driving this growth include the advancement of semiconductor manufacturing, miniaturization of electronics, and expanding use of digital imaging technologies in various sectors. The demand for high-resolution imaging, applications in medical devices, automotive electrification, and technological innovations in fabrication processes are key growth drivers. Market challenges include high material costs, regulatory constraints, and process complexity. Photosensitive films, liquid coatings, and photoresists are significant product types, used in diverse applications such as PCBs, OLED displays, and semiconductor lithography. The technology breakdown includes lithography dominating the market, especially in semiconductor and display manufacturing, and increasing demand for 3D printing applications with photopolymers. The market is segmented by application (consumer electronics, automotive, medical devices) and end-users (industrial, commercial, residential). More details available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Global digital photosensitive material market to grow from USD 6.2B in 2024 to USD 10.4B by 2034, with CAGR of 5.4%. Key drivers include demand in electronics, 3D printing, automotive, medical sectors, and technological advancements. Market faces restraints due to high raw material costs and regulatory pressures. Market divided into film, coating, and resist types, with applications in consumer electronics, automotive, and medical devices. Key technologies are lithography and 3D printing.

news.summary: The global rubber filling oil market, used as extenders and processing aids in rubber manufacturing, was valued at approximately USD3.1 billion in 2024 and is expected to reach around USD5.5 billion by 2034, growing at a CAGR of 6.1% during 2025–2034. This growth is driven by demand from the automotive sector, industrial applications, and a shift towards sustainable rubber processing methods. The industry faces challenges such as stringent environmental regulations, volatile crude oil prices, and competition from alternative materials. Key market players include Royal Dutch Shell, ExxonMobil, TotalEnergies, BP, Chevron, Indian Oil Corporation, PetroChina, Sinopec, H&R Group, Nynas, and Petronas. Asia-Pacific leads the market, accounting for 45% of global revenue, driven by China, India, and Southeast Asian countries. North America and Europe hold 25% and 20% market share respectively. This growth is fuelled by booming automotive production, industrial expansion, and advancements in rubber processing technology.
news.headline: Global rubber filling oil market valued at USD 3.1 billion in 2024, expected to reach USD 5.5 billion by 2034, growing at 6.1% CAGR. Exciting growth driven by automotive sector, industrial expansion, and sustainable rubber processing. Market players include Shell, ExxonMobil, TotalEnergies, BP, Chevron, IOCL, PetroChina, Sinopec, and regional & specialized firms. Asia-Pacific leads with 45% revenue.

news.summary: Genome & Company, a leading biotechnology firm, recently made a significant move by introducing its skincare brand UIQ to the global market. The launch marks the company's full-scale entry into international markets. Their product, Biome Barrier Cream Mist, has received high accolades and even secured the 'Amazon's Choice' designation. This development took place on May 16, 2025, in Seoul, South Korea. Genome & Company is well-positioned to capitalize on this success and make a mark in the competitive global skincare industry.
news.headline: Genome & Company expands skincare brand UIQ to North America, launching an Amazon store; earns 'Amazon's Choice' title.
